_meta.purpose: Compact summary of all 8 experiments + extensions, for non-interactive sections of the landing page (table, badges, footer)
_meta.hardware: Single Tesla V100S 32GB · Aldan3 cluster (Pirogov RNIMU)

experiments.title: Layer-wise probing (bert-base, 13 layers)
experiments.outcome: Mid-layer peak (L4–L5) for promoter and coding; near-chance at all layers for enhancer/OCR.
experiments.closes_weakness: Extends Fig 4D of the paper with task-resolved layer-wise view; foundation for E3-v2 and E4.

experiments.title: GENA-LM vs HyenaDNA (last layer)
experiments.outcome: Naïve last-layer comparison: HyenaDNA wins 3/4 (Promoter, Enhancer, OCR); GENA-LM wins on Coding.
experiments.closes_weakness: W7 (no SSM comparison) + W12 (no DART-Eval-style critique).
experiments.scope: Frozen-probing on short-range regulatory tasks only.

experiments.title: GENA-LM @ E1-best layer per task
experiments.outcome: Re-running with best layer per task: GENA-LM wins on Promoter (L4) and Coding (L5), ties on OCR, loses on Enhancer. Flips the head-to-head narrative.
experiments.lesson: The naïve 'HyenaDNA wins 3/4' result is partly a layer-choice artefact. Practical guideline from E4 — do not query the last layer if not fine-tuning — is the durable finding.

experiments.title: Multilayer UMAP clustering (L0/L4/L12)
experiments.outcome: Best silhouette at L4 (0.164) vs L0 (0.111), L12 (0.124). NMI flat across layers — total information preserved, geometry differs.

experiments.title: In silico CTCF saturation mutagenesis + negative control
experiments.outcome: Pearson r = 0.893 between substitution effect and (1 − PWM_prob). Negative control at non-motif positions gives 15× smaller effect (0.0004 vs 0.0060).
experiments.closes_weakness: W10 (token importance ≠ causality) with a positive, counterfactual result.

experiments.title: DART-Eval-inspired 4-way (OCR proxy)
experiments.outcome: GENA-LM (110.7M, frozen) AUROC=0.658 vs 3-mer+LogReg (65 features) 0.647 — +1.1% from a 110M-param foundation model. HyenaDNA wins overall (0.688). TinyCNN (104k params, supervised) ties GENA-LM.
experiments.closes_weakness: W12 (no DART-Eval comparison) — proxy task, formal DART-Eval is natural follow-up.

experiments.title: Layer-wise probing on bert-large (336M, 24 layers)
experiments.outcome: Mid-layer pattern replicates at proportional depth (L8–L11 of 24). Enhancer F1 jumps 0.66 → 0.73 (+0.07), OCR 0.62 → 0.67 (+0.05). Long-range tasks need scale or longer context.

experiments.title: GENA-LM fine-tuned on promoter (calibration)
experiments.outcome: 3-epoch fine-tune, 512-token windows, 3 seeds: F1 = 0.794 ± 0.004. Frozen best-layer (0.808) is actually higher; gap to authors' published 0.94 (with 2 kb context, longer training, likely bert-large) sets scope on our frozen-probing claims.

limitations_scope: Frozen-probing only — authors' published fine-tuned numbers are substantially higher.
E6 uses an OCR-based proxy, not the formal DART-Eval Task 1–5 pipeline (Synapse access required).
Single GENA-LM variant tested (bert-base-t2t, 4.5 kb max). BigBird/RMT variants — where GENA-LM has architectural advantage on long-range tasks — are not tested.
Single HyenaDNA variant (tiny-1k-seqlen, 0.44 M); larger variants may compress or invert the gap.
Short-range tasks only in head-to-head (≤ 1–2 kb). Long-range tasks where GENA-LM is documented to outperform HyenaDNA are deliberately not included.
Per-task sample sizes 2k–5k limit statistical power; F1 deltas of 0.01–0.05 are near seed-noise resolution.

E5 is counterfactual in silico — stronger than correlational attribution but weaker than functional perturbation in a reporter assay. Negative control (15× smaller effect at random non-motif positions) rules out generic BPE-tokenisation sensitivity.

W6.name: Sample efficiency in cross-species transfer unmeasured

W7.name: No comparison with SSM (Caduceus) or modern alternatives

W12.name: No evaluation on DART-Eval (NeurIPS 2024) benchmark
W12.note: DART-Eval-inspired proxy; formal benchmark is follow-up.
